title: TRIM29 promotes epithelial–mesenchymal transition, angiogenesis, and stromal remodeling in lung adenocarcinoma: integrated validation at histologic, transcriptomic, and protein levels
task: Determine TRIM29's role in lung adenocarcinoma progression and tumor microenvironment remodeling
key_results: TRIM29-high tumors show enriched EMT signatures, increased fibroblasts/endothelial cells, and poor overall survival; mTOR inhibitors demonstrate highest activity in TRIM29-high cells
comments: Comprehensive multi-level validation (transcriptomic, histologic, proteomic) strengthens findings, though causal mechanisms of TRIM29-driven EMT warrant further investigation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: High TRIM29 expression drives EMT, angiogenesis, and stromal remodeling in LUAD
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via GSEA enrichment, deconvolution analysis, and immunohistochemistry correlations

hypotheses.hypothesis: TRIM29 promotes EMT through upregulation of SNAIL and TWIST
hypotheses.result: Supported; TRIM29 inhibition decreased SNAIL/TWIST and increased E-cadherin

hypotheses.hypothesis: TRIM29-high tumors respond to mTOR inhibition
hypotheses.result: mTOR inhibitors showed highest drug sensitivity in TRIM29-high cells

title: Enhancing survival risk prediction through imputation and feature selection in high-dimensional protein biomarker data
task: Survival risk prediction from high-dimensional protein biomarker data with missing values
key_results: Pipeline integrates random forest imputation, LASSO Cox regression, and random survival forests; demonstrates biomarker stability assessment in small-sample cancer proteomic dataset
comments: Workflow is reproducible and transparently presented, but explicitly acknowledged as best-practice application rather than novel methodology; overfitting risk in high-dimensional settings remains a key challenge.

model_io.model: Sequential biomarker pipeline: imputation → feature selection → survival modeling
inputs: proteomic data with missing values, clinical event times
outputs: selected biomarkers, risk scores, Cox proportional hazards estimates

hypotheses.hypothesis: Outcome-blind random forest imputation reduces information leakage compared to outcome-informed methods
hypotheses.result: Supported conceptually; empirical comparison not provided in abstract

title: Design, synthesis, and in vivo antiepileptic evaluation of novel quinazolinone-phthalimide derivatives
task: Synthesis and in vivo antiepileptic evaluation of quinazolinone-phthalimide derivatives
key_results: Compounds 8j and 8k show seizure latency and count comparable to diazepam in two animal models; no mortality observed; docking studies confirm GABAA receptor binding; molecular dynamics confirm complex stability
comments: Medicinal chemistry study with promising empirical results; mechanism of GABAA antagonism needs clarification given claims of GABAergic activity; limited to animal models.

model_io.model: Drug discovery pipeline: rational design → synthesis → characterization → in vivo testing → molecular modeling
inputs: quinazolinone-phthalimide scaffolds, pentylenetetrazole and lithium-pilocarpine seizure induction protocols
outputs: seizure latency, seizure count, mortality, GABAA binding interactions, docking scores, MD trajectories

hypotheses.hypothesis: Quinazolinone-phthalimide compounds 8j and 8k have antiepileptic efficacy equivalent to diazepam via GABAA receptor interaction
hypotheses.result: Supported: comparable seizure suppression and GABAA binding predicted; clinical translation untested

title: Mechanistic role of gut microbiota metabolites in hypertension-insomnia comorbidity via integrated network pharmacology and molecular dynamics
task: Mechanistic role of gut microbiota metabolites in hypertension-insomnia comorbidity
key_results: 18 core targets identified (IL6, PPARG as hubs with 12 connections each); IL-17 signaling pathway crucial; 3-indolepropionic acid binds IL6 (Vina score -5.472 kcal/mol) with stable dynamics; butyrate and 3-indolepropionic acid show promising drug-likeness profiles
comments: Integrative pharmacology approach spanning 278 metabolites and 408 common targets; molecular docking and MD simulations support binding predictions, but causal role of metabolites in comorbidity mechanism remains inferential.

model_io.model: Integrated network pharmacology: metabolite-target prediction → PPI analysis → KEGG enrichment → molecular docking → MD simulation → toxicity assessment
inputs: 278 gut microbial metabolites (gutMGene), 13,700 hypertension and 502 insomnia targets, IL6/PPARG structures
outputs: core target genes, pathway enrichments (IL-17 signaling), docking poses, MD stability metrics, drug-likeness scores

hypotheses.hypothesis: Gut microbiota metabolites regulate shared hypertension-insomnia targets via IL-17 signaling and neuroinflammation
hypotheses.result: Supported by network analysis: IL-17 pathway highlighted; molecular interactions predicted

hypotheses.hypothesis: 3-indolepropionic acid and butyrate are promising therapeutic candidates for hypertension-insomnia comorbidity
hypotheses.result: Partially supported: binding affinity and drug-likeness favorable, but in vivo efficacy untested

title: Variance-calibrated cross-individual bootstrapping for small-sample neuroscience
task: Robust confidence interval estimation for small-sample neuroscience data
key_results: CIB-VC achieves near-nominal 95% coverage across distributions; 23% narrower confidence intervals than hierarchical bootstrapping on fish behavioral data (N=5)
comments: Addresses a genuine practical problem in neuroscience, though validation is limited to one empirical dataset; Monte Carlo simulations are comprehensive but domain-specific application breadth unclear.

inputs: small-sample datasets with within-subject trial structures
outputs: confidence intervals with empirical coverage rates

hypotheses.hypothesis: CIB-VC achieves better coverage-precision balance than stratified and hierarchical bootstrapping under skewed distributions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: near-nominal coverage vs. <1% for stratified under skew

title: Single cell profiling of ER stress in coronary artery disease and therapeutic mechanisms of Ginkgo biloba extract
task: Identification of ER stress-related genes in coronary artery disease and therapeutic validation
key_results: 833 DEGs and 10 ERSRGs identified; Ginkgo biloba extract (GBE) predicted as top therapeutic compound and validated in cell experiments; scRNA-seq revealed cell-type specificity of ERSRGs
comments: Comprehensive integrative approach spanning transcriptomics, immune profiling, drug prediction, and functional validation, but clinical translation remains speculative; GBE mechanism in ER stress requires deeper mechanistic investigation.

model_io.model: Multi-omics pipeline: differential expression → functional enrichment → immune deconvolution → drug prediction → scRNA-seq
inputs: gene expression data (GEO), ER stress gene set, immune algorithm reference matrices
outputs: ERSRGs, risk scores, therapeutic candidates, cell-type gene associations

hypotheses.hypothesis: ER stress markers are associated with CAD pathogenesis and immune infiltration patterns
hypotheses.result: Supported: 10 ERSRGs identified with notable immune cell infiltration differences

hypotheses.hypothesis: GBE regulates ER stress in CAD cells
hypotheses.result: Supported: cell experiments confirmed GBE effect on ER stress

title: Supervised machine learning intrusion detection review and multi-criteria evaluation
task: Multi-criteria evaluation framework for supervised machine learning intrusion detection systems
key_results: TOPSIS-based evaluation on three datasets (KDD, NSL-KDD, CICIDS2017); tree-based methods (Random Tree, C4.5, Random Forest) consistently rank highest; Naïve Bayes performs worst
comments: Systematic review and evaluation framework is transparent; results dataset-dependent and may not generalize to contemporary attack patterns; no temporal validation across dataset shifts.

model_io.model: Supervised ML IDS evaluation pipeline: algorithm selection → dataset preprocessing → classification → multi-criteria ranking
inputs: network traffic features (three IDS datasets), labeled attack/normal flows
outputs: algorithm performance metrics (detection rate, false positive rate), TOPSIS rankings with multiple evaluator preferences

hypotheses.hypothesis: Tree-based classification algorithms outperform other supervised methods for IDS across multiple datasets and evaluator preferences
hypotheses.result: Supported: tree methods consistently achieve top TOPSIS rankings

title: Persistent cycles and network resilience: a hypernetwork-based framework for temporal graph analysis
task: Temporal network resilience analysis via persistent cycle detection in hypernetwork framework
key_results: Temporal Cycle Number (TCN) and Temporal Cycle Ratio (TCR) metrics exhibit higher rank association with disruption impact than static/temporal baselines on 6 real-world networks; targeted TCN removal yields larger efficiency degradation than degree-based attacks
comments: Novel and theoretically grounded framework; empirical validation across diverse domains is comprehensive, but sensitivity to windowing parameters and persistence thresholds not thoroughly explored.

model_io.model: Temporal hypernetwork pipeline: sliding-window cycle detection → persistence tracking → TCN/TCR metrics → resilience evaluation
inputs: temporal networks (time-stamped interaction events across 6 domains)
window size and persistence threshold parameters
outputs: persistent cycle hyperedges, TCN/TCR metrics, disruption impact scores, network efficiency loss

hypotheses.hypothesis: Persistent cycles in temporal networks are more predictive of resilience than static or temporal baseline metrics
hypotheses.result: Supported: TCN/TCR show higher rank association with disruption impact than degree/temporal baselines

hypotheses.hypothesis: Topological closure (cycles) rather than motif persistence alone drives resilience prediction
hypotheses.result: Supported: persistent cycles outperform non-closed 2-path motifs in predictive power

title: Validation of conformal prediction in cervical atypia classification
task: Validation of conformal prediction for uncertainty quantification in cervical atypia classification
key_results: Comprehensive expert annotation-based validation reveals conventional coverage metrics overestimate performance; conformal prediction sets often misaligned with human labels; methods successfully identify ambiguous and OOD data
comments: Important methodological critique showing conformal prediction's limitation in practical healthcare settings; highlights gap between statistical validity and clinical usability.

model_io.model: Conformal prediction applied to three deep learning models for cervical atypia classification
inputs: cervical cytology images, expert multi-annotator labels
outputs: prediction sets with coverage guarantees, set size (uncertainty), OOD detection flags

hypotheses.hypothesis: Conformal prediction provides valid uncertainty quantification aligned with human expert judgement
hypotheses.result: Partially rejected: conventional conformal prediction overestimates coverage and produces misaligned prediction sets

title: A contrastive learning framework with adaptive feature fusion for brain tumor classification
task: Brain tumor classification from MRI via contrastive learning with adaptive feature fusion
key_results: AFF-CL framework achieves state-of-the-art performance on figshare brain tumor dataset; dynamic label queue generates multiple positive pairs; adaptive feature fusion integrates local and global MRI features
comments: Novel contrastive learning design with intuitive motivation; quantitative performance metrics (accuracy, AUC) absent from abstract; generalization to other tumor types or imaging modalities unclear.

model_io.model: Contrastive learning framework with adaptive feature fusion (AFF-CL)
inputs: brain MRI scans, tumor class labels (multi-class classification)
outputs: tumor class predictions, learned feature representations, per-tumor classification confidence

hypotheses.hypothesis: Contrastive learning with multiple positive pairs improves intra-class compactness and inter-class separation for brain tumor classification
hypotheses.result: Supported: state-of-the-art performance achieved; detailed metrics unavailable in abstract

hypotheses.hypothesis: Adaptive feature fusion capturing multi-scale contextual information enhances classification discriminative power
hypotheses.result: Supported conceptually; quantitative ablation not detailed in abstract

title: The explainability of radiomic-based machine learning models for brain glioma grading on amide proton transfer-weighted images
task: Explainability of radiomic ML models for brain glioma grading on APTw MRI
key_results: Random forest, SVM, naïve Bayes, and logistic regression models trained on APTw radiomic features; explainability analysis via Shapley values, permutation importance, and anchors revealed feature heterogeneity dependence
comments: Applies established explainability methods to radiomics but lacks explicit performance metrics (accuracy, AUC); clinical utility of heterogeneity insights unclear.

model_io.model: Radiomic ML pipeline with explainability: feature extraction → classification → interpretation
inputs: APTw MRI images (contrast-enhanced and peritumoral regions), tumor grade labels
outputs: grade predictions (grade 4 vs. non-grade 4), Shapley values, feature importance rankings

hypotheses.hypothesis: Radiomic features capturing tumor heterogeneity are primary drivers of glioma grading models
hypotheses.result: Supported: explainability analysis highlighted heterogeneity-related features

title: Characterization of T-cell receptor loci and expressed repertoire reveals a capacity for robust T-cell response in Atlantic cod (Gadus morhua)
task: Characterization of T-cell receptor repertoire diversity in Atlantic cod immune system
key_results: All four TCR chains annotated in cod genome; modest germline diversity but surprisingly high expressed repertoire diversity (TRA, TRB, TRD chains); mostly private repertoire with high untapped diversity
comments: Solid genomic and immunological characterization providing baseline for fish immunology; functional validation of repertoire-disease associations absent.

model_io.model: TCR annotation and repertoire sequencing analysis
inputs: Atlantic cod genome (gadmor3.0), expressed TCR sequences
outputs: germline TCR loci composition, repertoire diversity metrics, public vs. private TCR fractions

hypotheses.hypothesis: Despite loss of MHCII/CD4, Atlantic cod maintains effective immune capacity via diverse TCR repertoire
hypotheses.result: Supported: high expressed repertoire diversity observed despite modest germline diversity

title: Automated kidney stone segmentation from flexible ureteroscopy videos using a U-net model: A preliminary feasibility study
task: Deep learning segmentation of kidney stones from flexible ureteroscopy video
key_results: U-Net with DenseNet-121 encoder achieves 92-94% pixel accuracy and 73-76% Dice coefficient on test set; 30 fps inference speed; degraded performance under bleeding, dust, motion blur
comments: Feasibility study with small dataset (12 videos, ~11h) and single-center origin; clinical integration and multi-center validation essential before deployment.

model_io.model: 2D U-Net with DenseNet-121 encoder for kidney stone segmentation
inputs: ureteroscopy video frames (annotated kidney stone regions)
outputs: pixel-level stone segmentation masks, inference time per frame

hypotheses.hypothesis: Deep learning can accurately segment kidney stones from ureteroscopic video under real-world conditions
hypotheses.result: Partially supported: high accuracy on clear visibility; degraded under challenging visual conditions

title: Screenathon 2.0: human–AI collaborative screening applied to patient-generated health data
task: Crowd-sourced human-AI collaborative screening for systematic reviews
key_results: 5,842 papers screened across 11 disease topics in 2 days by 27 collaborators; 487 relevant, 6,463 irrelevant records; post-hoc survey showed increased trust in AI-assisted screening
comments: Demonstrates operational feasibility but lacks quantitative validation metrics for screening accuracy or comparison to traditional methods; post-processing safeguards appear ad hoc.

model_io.model: Crowd-based screening with adaptive machine learning and LLM integration
outputs: relevance labels (relevant/irrelevant), with survey feedback on usability and trust

hypotheses.hypothesis: Human-AI collaboration improves systematic review screening speed and user trust relative to human-only approaches
hypotheses.result: Partially supported: operational speed demonstrated; trust increase shown via survey but no baseline comparison

title: Identification of key genes in the pathogenesis of hepatic ischemia-reperfusion injury based on bioinformatics and experimental verification
task: Identification of key genes in hepatic ischemia-reperfusion injury and therapeutic targets
key_results: 115 candidate genes identified; three key genes (Adh4, Akr1c14, Cxcl1) prioritized via PPI and ML; enriched in aerobic respiration and mitochondrial pathways; ladarixin (CXCR1/2 antagonist) showed protective effect
comments: Integrative bioinformatics approach with experimental verification; mechanistic understanding of identified genes incomplete; therapeutic claim based on single agent test.

model_io.model: Gene discovery pipeline: whole-transcriptome sequencing → differential expression → PPI network → ML prioritization → immune deconvolution → molecular networks → target validation
inputs: mouse HIRI model RNA-seq data, protein interaction databases, immune profiling databases
outputs: key genes (Adh4, Akr1c14, Cxcl1), regulatory networks, immune infiltration patterns, drug candidate predictions

hypotheses.hypothesis: Key genes identified via integrated genomics analysis are functional drivers of HIRI pathogenesis
hypotheses.result: Partially supported: transcriptional and translational verification provided; functional validation incomplete

hypotheses.hypothesis: CXCR1/2 inhibition via ladarixin protects against HIRI
hypotheses.result: Supported: ladarixin protective effect demonstrated; mechanism requires deeper investigation

title: A hybrid recommendation framework utilizing domain-adaptive RoBERTa embeddings for enhanced personalization in e-commerce
task: Hybrid recommendation framework with domain-adaptive RoBERTa and attention-fused embeddings for e-commerce
key_results: HyReC achieves RMSE 0.15, MAE 0.10, MSE 0.023, R² 0.98, Pearson correlation 0.99, MAPE 1.5%, F1 0.98 on Amazon Baby dataset; outperforms LSTM, RBM+KNN, GNN, GAT baselines
comments: Comprehensive hybrid approach with semantic + behavioral + statistical signals; strong experimental metrics; evaluation on single e-commerce domain; hyperparameter sensitivity not discussed.

inputs: behavioral features (mean rating, variance, frequency, skewness)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Heterogeneous embedding fusion with Bahdanau attention outperforms single-signal recommendation approaches
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; superior performance on Amazon Baby dataset vs. state-of-the-art baselines

title: Real-time decentralized model predictive control for cooperative multi-robot object transport: experimental validation
task: Decentralized model predictive control for multi-robot cooperative object transport
key_results: Real-time MPC with nonlinear kinematics and inter-robot coupling; validated on two mobile robots; accurate trajectory tracking across point-to-point, curvilinear, and obstacle-rich scenarios
comments: Solid experimental validation on physical testbed; strength in constraint satisfaction; limited scalability analysis beyond two robots.

outputs: velocity commands for two differential-drive robots

hypotheses.hypothesis: Decentralized MPC with adaptive cost weighting enables real-time multi-robot coordination
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; robustness to environmental uncertainties demonstrated experimentally

title: Design of an in-pipe inspection robotic system (IPIRS) with YOLOv8–LSTM integration for real-time in-pipe navigation
task: In-pipe inspection with YOLOv8-LSTM integration for robotic navigation and object detection
key_results: YOLOv8 achieves mAP(0.5) 97.9% and F1 0.95; LSTM motor voltage prediction: MSE 0.00037, MAE 0.00581; navigation tested on 100–150 mm diameter pipes
comments: Well-engineered pipeline combining perception and control; strong performance metrics; limited discussion of failure modes in extreme curvature or debris occlusion.

outputs: object bounding boxes and class predictions
motor voltage commands for smooth navigation

hypotheses.hypothesis: YOLOv8-LSTM architecture improves inspection accuracy and enables predictive maintenance in curved pipes
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via simulation and controlled testbed experiments

title: A low-latency deep learning framework for volcanic ash cloud nowcasting using geostationary satellite imagery
task: Low-latency nowcasting of volcanic ash dispersion from satellite imagery
key_results: Structural similarity index of 0.88 for 15-minute forecasts; complete edge workflow runs in <5 seconds on NVIDIA Jetson; demonstrates scenario injection for synthetic plume visualization
comments: Strong focus on deployment efficiency and practical utility; scenario outputs explicitly not validated as true nuclear plume predictions, limiting extrapolation claims.

inputs: SEVIRI Ash RGB composite satellite imagery
synthetic plume parameters (10 kt–100 Mt yield-inspired sizes)
outputs: overlaid satellite imagery with synthetic plumes for scenario visualization

hypotheses.hypothesis: Deep learning can achieve near-real-time nowcasting on edge hardware with acceptable fidelity
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SSIM 0.88 achieved within 5-second end-to-end latency

title: An advanced fermatean fuzzy DoC MCDM architecture for comprehensive quantitative assessment of physical fitness competency across academic institutions
task: Fermatean Fuzzy Deck-of-Cards MCDM for student physical fitness assessment
key_results: FF-DoC framework produces consistent and explainable fitness competency rankings using incomplete/uncertain expert opinions; criteria: endurance, strength, flexibility, body composition, mental well-being
comments: High expressiveness of Fermatean Fuzzy sets is theoretical advantage; Deck-of-Cards weighting improves transparency; hypothetical case study only; lacks empirical validation on real institutional data.

inputs: expert assessments of fitness criteria (endurance, strength, flexibility, body composition, mental well-being) under uncertainty
outputs: ranked fitness competency scores for students/institutions

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fermatean Fuzzy sets with DoC weighting better capture expert hesitation and dual uncertainty than FS/IFS/PFS
hypotheses.result: Theoretically motivated; empirical validation on real case study pending

title: Fault diagnosis method of rolling-element bearings via FBEWT and EIRVM
task: Bearing fault diagnosis via Fourier-Bessel empirical wavelet transform and enhanced incremental RVM
key_results: FBEWT with amplitude-modulation-based frequency boundary setting outperforms conventional empirical wavelet decomposition; EIRVM surpasses standard RVM; integrated FBEWT-EIRVM demonstrates strong diagnostic capability
comments: Incremental improvement via signal processing and classifier enhancement; limited quantitative benchmarking (no reported accuracy/F1 metrics provided in abstract).

hypotheses.hypothesis: Amplitude-modulation-based frequency boundaries improve FBEWT robustness across sampling rates and observation windows
hypotheses.result: Supported; yields stable results under parameter variations

title: DMSTG-AD: an SDN intrusion detection method based on dynamic multi-scale spatio-temporal graph neural network
task: Dynamic multi-scale spatio-temporal graph neural network for SDN intrusion detection
key_results: DMSTG-AD achieves 99.34% multi-class accuracy on CIC-IDS2017 and 99.88% on InSDN; GRU-driven dynamic embeddings, adaptive adjacency, multi-scale dilated convolution, and cross-attention mechanism all validated via ablation
comments: Comprehensive architecture with well-ablated components; very high reported accuracies typical of IDS benchmarks; limited discussion of false-positive rates or deployment constraints.

model_io.model: DMSTG-AD (Dynamic Multi-Scale Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Network)
inputs: network traffic flow data as dynamic graph

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dynamic graph modeling with multi-scale temporal convolution and cross-attention improves SDN anomaly detection
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ablation validates critical importance of each component

title: Lightweight FMCW radar framework for human activity recognition under limited data conditions
task: Lightweight FMCW radar framework for human activity recognition on edge devices with limited training data
key_results: ResNet-18 with depthwise separable convolutions + bidirectional LSTM achieves 91.98% accuracy and 89.82% F1-score on 60 GHz dataset; superior performance in cross-scene and leave-one-person-out validation
comments: Practical edge deployment focus with thoughtful data augmentation; modest dataset scale (60 GHz, 7 activities); generalization to other frequency bands and activity sets unclear.

inputs: Range-Doppler, Range-Azimuth, Range-Elevation feature maps from 60 GHz FMCW radar

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-dimensional radar feature representation with data augmentation enables robust HAR under limited training conditions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; outperforms baselines in cross-scene and user-invariance tests

title: General two-level framework for demand-responsive transport optimization
task: Demand-responsive transport optimization with vehicle energy and passenger constraints
key_results: Two-level optimization framework tested on Rzeszow Poland dataset (64 bus stops, 200 passengers); three variants (General, Sections, Routes) evaluated; effectiveness confirmed for basic and extended versions
comments: Practical framework with good scalability structure; evaluation limited to single city; impact of extensions (EV charging, accessibility) on solution quality not quantitatively decomposed.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hierarchical DRT framework with energy and special-request constraints is computationally tractable
hypotheses.result: Confirmed on 200-passenger test instance

title: Deep maximum margin matrix factorization
task: Deep maximum margin matrix factorization for collaborative filtering on ordinal feedback
key_results: Deep MMMF variant outperforms state-of-the-art CF methods in NMAE metric across three benchmark datasets; addresses non-linear user taste patterns
comments: Clear motivation (replacing linear hyperplane with deep model); limited detail on experimental setup and baseline comparisons in abstract; generalization claims need stronger validation.

model_io.model: Deep Maximum Margin Matrix Factorization (Deep MMMF)
inputs: partially observed user-item ordinal rating matrix

hypotheses.hypothesis: Deep nonlinear embedding of users outperforms linear hyperplane MMMF for complex taste patterns
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via NMAE metric improvements on benchmarks

title: A misclassification-aware explainable hybrid CNN-vision transformer framework for radiographic weld inspection
task: Hybrid CNN-Vision Transformer for explainable radiographic weld defect classification
key_results: Hybrid CNN-ViT achieves 98.56% accuracy vs. 97.90% CNN baseline (~31% relative misclassification reduction); Grad-CAM and self-attention maps explain similar-defect discrimination; cross-dataset validation on GDXray confirms robustness
comments: Solid multi-faceted evaluation including explainability and cross-dataset generalization; practical relevance to Industry 5.0; modest accuracy gain but meaningful misclassification reduction.

outputs: defect class predictions with attention maps

hypotheses.hypothesis: Global contextual modeling via transformers improves discrimination of visually similar weld defects
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; cracks, porosity, and absence of penetration better distinguished via CNN-ViT

title: Exploring vision transformers for deep feature extraction and classification in video genre recognition for digital media
task: TV genre classification using vision transformers
key_results: PvT achieves 97% accuracy on static image dataset; MAiVAR-T achieves 98% accuracy on multimodal audio-video dataset
comments: Solid application of transformer architectures to media analysis, though claims of 'state-of-the-art' lack comparison with recent multimodal baselines.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Transformer-based models capture spatio-temporal and acoustic relationships better than traditional deep learning
hypotheses.result: Empirically confirmed; PvT and MAiVAR-T outperform baseline deep learning models

title: Human-centric participation paradigm: exploring the enjoyment experience of cultural heritage virtual museum based on system usability and VR technology
task: Model user enjoyment in virtual cultural heritage museums
key_results: Immersion exerts strongest effect on enjoyment; basic psychological needs moderate perceived usefulness-enjoyment link; 630-user survey via PLS-SEM
comments: Well-structured theoretical integration but relies entirely on self-reported survey data with potential common-method bias; causality claims are speculative.

model_io.model: Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM)
inputs: survey responses from 630 Virtual Palace Museum users
outputs: path coefficients for system usability → psychological constructs → enjoyment

hypotheses.hypothesis: System usability enhances perceived usefulness, curiosity, joy, control, and immersion
hypotheses.result: Confirmed for all five constructs

hypotheses.hypothesis: Immersion has strongest direct effect on enjoyment
hypotheses.result: Confirmed

hypotheses.hypothesis: Control significantly impacts enjoyment
hypotheses.result: Rejected—no significant effect found

title: A quantile-based composite ionospheric disturbance estimator for RTK positioning reliability
task: Composite ionospheric disturbance estimation for RTK positioning reliability via quantile-based metrics
key_results: Framework integrates temporal ROTI variability and spatial VTEC gradient into unified disturbance estimator; validated on mid-latitude regional CORS network (Latvia); demonstrates physically interpretable risk scoring
comments: Methodologically sound quantile-based approach; validation limited to single region (Latvia); extension to sparse networks acknowledged as future work.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Composite indicator combining temporal and spatial ionospheric measures better captures disturbance than single-parameter metrics
hypotheses.result: Supported; captures patterns not resolved by global ionospheric models

title: Teaching activity design and psychological practice of music majors under a convolutional neural network and transformer module
task: Personalized music teaching activity recommendation via CNN-Transformer under STEAM education
key_results: CNN-Transformer achieves accuracy 95.76%, recall 90.28%, F1 0.92; outperforms references by >4%; user engagement (clicks, browsing time, comments) increased >10%
comments: Decent performance uplift; STEAM context is motivational but not technically novel; user engagement metrics are suggestive rather than rigorous learning outcome measures.

hypotheses.hypothesis: CNN-Transformer outperforms reference models in prediction accuracy and user engagement
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 95.76% accuracy and >10% engagement lift observed

title: DFT based photophysical assessment of 2-substituted-3-(pyridin-2-yl)-benzo-[d][1,3]-azaphosphole P-oxide for organic optoelectronic applications
task: DFT design and characterization of phosphole-based organic semiconductors with tunable push-pull architecture
key_results: π-extended heteroacene derivatives exhibit tunable emission (367–515 nm), high oscillator strengths (f up to 0.793), enhanced nonlinear optical response (β up to 2621 a.u.), and favorable hole transport (λh = 0.20–0.28 eV); compounds 6, 7, 8 identified as priority candidates with cyan, blue, and orange emission respectively
comments: Fragment orbital analysis rigorously connects structure to electronic reconfiguration, but predictions remain computational; experimental validation of photophysical and transport properties is essential.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pyridin-2-yl substitution inverts intramolecular charge-transfer pathway relative to phenyl analogs
hypotheses.result: Pyridine ring contributes 52–75% HOMO, aryl substituent contributes 33–59% LUMO, establishing push-pull framework

hypotheses.hypothesis: Greater HOMO-LUMO spatial separation systematically modulates optoelectronic properties
hypotheses.result: Orbital separation correlates with red-shifted emission, lowered reorganization energy, narrowed ΔEST, and increased hyperpolarizability across the series

title: Experimental and Monte Carlo simulation study on photons shielding properties of ZrO2-reinforced polyester composites utilizing GEANT4 and MCNP codes
task: Gamma-ray shielding properties of ZrO₂-reinforced polyester composites
key_results: Good agreement between GEANT4/MCNPX Monte Carlo simulations and experimental data at 662 keV (137Cs); lead equivalent thickness increases monotonically with ZrO₂ filler content
comments: Multi-code validation (GEANT4, MCNPX, XCOM, Phy-X, EpiXS, NGCal) strengthens credibility, but the study lacks quantitative shielding performance thresholds or cost-benefit analysis versus traditional materials.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Increasing ZrO₂ filler content improves gamma-ray shielding performance
hypotheses.result: Shielding performance of PE/ZrO₂ composites improves with increasing ZrO₂ content; lead equivalent thickness increases with filler content

title: Decoupling in a joint communication and sensing system with metasurface
task: Decoupling self-interference in joint communication and sensing via metasurface
key_results: Metasurface-assisted decoupling using modified split-ring resonators (MSRRs) with null-space projection beamforming demonstrated in 2×2 patch antenna array at 9–10 GHz
comments: Hardware–algorithm co-optimization is pragmatic, but the paper lacks quantitative isolation/SI reduction metrics and validation on larger or more realistic MIMO geometries.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Metasurface suppresses surface currents to reduce TX-RX coupling in compact MIMO arrays
hypotheses.result: Measurement results validate effectiveness of proposed decoupling approach
